scenario_results.reason: The lane advanced a gaming-specific slot-service item through readiness, execution, and closure only when machine, patron, payout, and approval evidence were explicit.
Missing jurisdiction, asset, patron, and lifecycle evidence kept the work blocked and produced an exception note rather than an unsupported service recommendation.
Completion was refused until the required handpay, complaint, incident, and approval artifacts existed, keeping lifecycle transitions reversible when evidence failed.
Conflicting machine-state and work-order records were treated as a rework or escalation condition and did not result in unsupported closure.
Out-of-scope comp and player-account authority was rejected cleanly and routed to the correct authorized owner.
Responsible-gaming and self-exclusion signals moved the case into escalation posture and preserved both patron-protection and authority boundaries.
A patron payout dispute was packaged for the correct dispute or surveillance path instead of being settled inside floor operations.
Missing fill-credit or table-opening evidence correctly blocked lifecycle progression and requested the actual gaming artifacts required.
Realistic casino terminology and artifact shapes produced an industry-specific service summary rather than generic hospitality output.
The lane rejected generic casino framing until jurisdiction, game vertical, and control context were explicit.
Conflicting source and policy signals correctly triggered jurisdiction-specific escalation instead of confident synthesis.
Slot floor, patron-account, and complaint paths were separated into control-specific handling with preserved assumptions and next owners.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required abilities are mapped and validated.
All 12 scenarios passed against a 0.92 minimum threshold for casino-operations handling, exception management, completion evidence, and gaming-specific boundary safety.
Functionality coverage is complete across shared, workflow-phase, and industry-profile abilities.
